---
title: "NSF Funding Disruption for Universities"
subtitle: "Tracking the April 30, 2025 freeze through award terminations"
author: "Emily Stern"
institute: "EPPS 6354 · Information Management · UT Dallas · Spring 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, stern.scss]
    slide-number: true
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    footer: "NSF Funding Disruption · Stern · Spring 2026"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Research question

> How has **NSF** grant activity to **academic institutions** changed since
> the [April 30, 2025 funding freeze]{.anchor-date} and the subsequent
> **EO 14332** *(Improving Oversight of Federal Grantmaking, Aug 7, 2025)* —
> across the lifecycle of awards **obligated**, **terminated**, and
> **reinstated** — and which **directorates, programs, and universities**
> absorbed the largest share of the disruption?

::: {.callout-stern}
**Scope note.** Earlier drafts of this project also tracked Grants.gov
opportunity *removals*. The schema still supports that audit trail
(`change_log`), but only one Grants.gov snapshot has been loaded, so
opportunity-level rescissions are not analyzed here.
:::


## Data sources

| Source | What it provides |
|---|---|
| **USAspending.gov** | Transaction-level NSF assistance data, FY23–FY26 — obligations, deobligations, terminations as recorded |
| **Grants.gov** | NSF opportunity catalog *(single snapshot — schema supports diffs, no diff loaded yet)* |
| **Grant Watch** | Citizen-science tracker of NSF terminations & reinstatements (Ross + Delaney) |

All three sources are joined into a Postgres schema on Neon and surfaced
through a Shiny dashboard.


## Schema {background-iframe="schema.html" background-interactive="true"}

[Open in new tab ↗](schema.html){.fullscreen-link target="_blank"}

::: {.notes}
Six tables joining Grants.gov, USAspending, and Grant Watch.
Not shown in the diagram: a `snapshots` + `change_log` audit pair —
schema-ready for future Grants.gov diff runs, not populated yet.
:::


## Querying the schema: key questions

| Question | Tables joined |
|---|---|
| Which awards were **terminated** under the new administration? | `awards` ⋈ `terminations` |
| Did terminated awards **reinstate**, and how fast? | `terminations` *(reinstated, reinstatement_date)* |
| Which **directorates** are most affected? | `transactions` ⋈ `agencies` *(NSF parent → 11 directorates)* |
| Which **universities** are most affected? | `awards` ⋈ `recipients` *(academic-institution filter)* |
| How is disruption changing **over time**? | `transactions` + `terminations` on a shared month timeline |
| *(Future)* Which opportunities **disappeared**? | `opportunities` ⋈ `change_log` *(empty until second snapshot)* |


## Methodology highlights

- **Anchor: April 30, 2025 funding freeze.** The operational shock; EO 14332 (Aug 7) formalizes oversight after the fact.
- **At-risk pool = awards live on Apr 30, 2025** — denominator is exogenous to the disruption itself.
- **Positive vs. negative obligations are kept separate.** Net obligation hides disruption ($100M new + $50M clawback ≠ "$50M"); views split `gross_obligated` from `gross_deobligated`.
- **Same-month FY23–25 baseline.** Controls for NSF's annual August spike / October trough; Feb 2024 TIP $151M administrative re-obligation excluded so it doesn't inflate the baseline.
- **Citizen-tracker undercount detection.** Awards with >$100K post-freeze net deobligation but *no* termination record are surfaced as possible untracked disruption.


## The dashboard {background-iframe="https://connect.posit.cloud/emilyrstern/content/019df1ae-8f70-b795-8b7a-2be95508a9fa" background-interactive="true"}

[Open in new tab ↗](https://connect.posit.cloud/emilyrstern/content/019df1ae-8f70-b795-8b7a-2be95508a9fa){.fullscreen-link target="_blank"}


## Differentiating contribution

This dashboard builds on **Grant Watch** (Ross + Delaney) and the **Urban
Institute's** July 2025 keyword-cancellation breakdown. It extends both with:

1. **NEW-obligation pre-award drop per directorate** — FY26 H1 vs. FY25 H1
2. **Same-month FY23–25 seasonal baseline** — separates disruption from the August/October cycle
3. **Reinstatement dynamics** post-cancellation — velocity, not just counts
4. **USAspending cross-validation** — surfacing likely tracker undercount


## Limitations & next steps

::: {.incremental}
- **Opportunity-level removals not yet detected.** `change_log` is a schema
  stub; would require a second Grants.gov snapshot and a diff job.
- **NSF only.** The proposal scoped five agencies; narrowed to NSF for depth.
  The schema generalizes — `agencies` is already a parent/child rollup table.
- **Termination data is citizen-curated.** Cross-validation against
  USAspending net-deobligations partially mitigates undercount.
- **Posit Connect free tier sleeps after idle** — the live demo above may
  take ~5–10 s to wake on first load.
:::


## References & AI use

**Data**

- USAspending.gov — Bulk Assistance extracts, agency code 049, downloaded 2026-04-06
- Grants.gov — NSF opportunity extract, downloaded 2026-04-29
- Grant Watch — Noam Ross (rOpenSci) & Scott Delaney (Harvard)

**AI use disclosure**

This project used generative AI tools (OpenAI GPT models, Anthropic Claude
models) to support writing, literature review, project planning, and database
schema design. Methodological decisions, policy interpretation, source
verification, and final wording were produced and evaluated by the author.
